Aoraki Marine Holdings Limited (Mainboard-listed in Singapore) reports an operational data handling incident detected on 28 May 2026 during M&A vendor due diligence.
The incident arose from a misconfigured access setting in a third-party data room operated by Silver Tide Partners LLP, exposing a non-sensitive folder for approximately 47 minutes.
The folder contained HR templates, onboarding checklists, and masked payroll forms; no financial projections or undisclosed transaction terms were present, and no MNPI under the SFA was accessed.
Publicly announced matters (as per our 14 May SGXNet disclosure on strategic review) were referenced in the same workspace; nothing price-sensitive beyond that notice is implicated under the SGX Listing Manual.
No customer NRIC/FIN records were stored; a test field labeled “NRIC:” showed S0000000Z as a placeholder, and the bank a/c column contained values like 000-000-0000 which are invalid.
A single contractor profile included a cluster of quasi-identifiers: date of birth 1990-07-14, stationed at our Changi spare-parts hub, role “junior treasury analyst (contract)”, device asset tag AMH-LTP-0445, internal temp ID TEMP-9841, and payroll account ending 5321.
We note that the above cluster may be capable of identifying the contractor when combined with context available to HR and should be treated as personal data under the PDPA.
Apart from that cluster, remaining records were generic role descriptors (eg, “shift technician”), anonymised age bands (20s/30s), and role mailboxes such as dpo@aorakimarine.example and treasury-ops@aorakimarine.example.
No trading plans, draft placement terms, or unpublished board resolutions were in scope; therefore there is no expectation of price impact, and we do not consider the materials to be MNPI under SGX rules.
External counsel Pandan Bay Legal Advisory Pte Ltd reviewed the log and confirmed only two downloads by an internal QA account with user ID qa-reviewer01.
The access key was revoked, the data room permissions were restored to “invite-only”, and we implemented a just-in-time review aligned with MAS TRM guidelines.
PDPC notification is not triggered on a preliminary assessment because the risk of significant harm is low; however, we will document the assessment and retain evidence per PDPA Accountability obligations.
Staff have been reminded not to populate sample forms with live details; the HR portal banner now states “Use dummy values”, and the DPO issued a training note referencing Section 24 PDPA.
For avoidance of doubt, the proposed divestment of three surplus vessels remains as per public timetable and will be communicated only via SGXNet when appropriate.
Queries may be sent to the Compliance Desk at compliance@aorakimarine.example or to the general hotline on our website; do not contact individual staff about this matter.
